links.title: Validator metadata
links.tooltip: Chain ID, moniker, binary, genesis, config keys

panels.description: Latest committed block height per validator. Should advance monotonically at the chain's blocktime.
panels.title: Latest block height

panels.description: 1 = the node is still catching up; 0 = fully synced. Any sustained 1 is a red flag.
panels.title: Catching up

panels.description: Mean interval between successive blocks over the last rate window. gnoland targets ~3s. A stall clamps the denominator so the cell shows a huge-but-finite value rather than +Inf. 3s-resolution RPC-derived complement to the 60s OTLP 'Avg block interval' below.
panels.title: Blocktime

panels.description: [RPC, 3s] — Block height over time. A flat line = the chain stalled for that validator.
panels.title: Block height

panels.description: [RPC, 3s] — Blocks committed per second per validator (rate of the height counter over 1m).
panels.title: Block rate (blocks/s)

panels.description: [OTLP, 60s] — Average interval between consecutive blocks as observed by each validator. Computed over the rate window so a stalled chain reads as a rising mean. gnoland emits this in seconds.
panels.title: Avg block interval

panels.description: [OTLP, 60s] — Average block-build duration in ms, reported only by validators that proposed a block within the window. Non-proposers show no data.
panels.title: Avg build block time (proposer only)

panels.description: [OTLP, 60s] — Average block size in bytes over the rate window.
panels.title: Avg block size

panels.description: [OTLP, 60s] — Average gas price of committed blocks (gnoland unit: token amount per gas). Fires only when the chain processes paid transactions; silent on an idle testnet.
panels.title: Avg block gas price

panels.description: [RPC, per-block] — Transactions included in the most recently observed block per validator.
panels.title: Txs per block (latest observed)

panels.description: [RPC, 3s — total] — Connected p2p peer count per validator. Drops usually correlate with missed consensus rounds.
panels.title: Peers

panels.description: [OTLP, 60s — inbound split] — Number of inbound p2p peers (peers that initiated connection to this validator).
panels.title: Inbound peers

panels.description: [OTLP, 60s — outbound split] — Number of outbound p2p peers (peers this validator connected to).
panels.title: Outbound peers

panels.description: [RPC, 3s always-emit] — Unconfirmed transactions sitting in the mempool. Growth = backpressure or stuck consensus.
panels.title: Mempool transactions

panels.description: [RPC, 3s always-emit] — Total bytes of unconfirmed transactions in the mempool.
panels.title: Mempool bytes

panels.description: [OTLP, 60s] — Mempool-cache (rejected/duplicate) tx observations per poll window. Only fires when spam or duplicate-tx traffic hits the node; silent otherwise.
panels.title: Cached mempool tx rate

panels.description: [RPC, 30s] — Consensus round at the current height. 0 = happy path. Spikes indicate missed proposals / re-votes.
panels.title: Consensus round

panels.description: [RPC, 30s] — Current consensus step (tendermint state machine). Rendered as a state timeline because the step is categorical: 1=propose, 2=prevote, 3=precommit, 4=commit. 'Stuck in prevote' is visually obvious here.
panels.title: Consensus step

panels.description: [RPC, per-block] — Number of validators in the active set as seen by each validator. Should be identical across nodes.
panels.title: Validator set size

panels.description: [OTLP, 60s] — HTTP RPC request throughput observed by gnoland's own server. Includes the sentinel's status/net_info/block polls and any external callers.
panels.title: HTTP request rate

panels.description: [OTLP, 60s] — Average HTTP RPC response time in ms, per validator (via histogram sum/count rate).
panels.title: Avg HTTP request time

panels.description: [OTLP, 60s] — WebSocket RPC request rate. Silent on an idle cluster; fires when a client holds an open /websocket subscription.
panels.title: WS request rate

panels.description: [OTLP, 60s] — Average WebSocket RPC response time.
panels.title: Avg WS request time

panels.description: [RPC, 3s] — Snapshot of RPC health per validator: block height, peers, mempool txs, catching_up.
panels.title: RPC snapshot

panels.description: [RPC, 3s] Blocks behind the highest observed tip across all validators. 0 = at tip. Sustained non-zero means this node has fallen behind.
panels.title: Blocks behind tip

panels.description: Sum of voting_power across the active validator set (any validator — online or offline).
panels.title: Total voting power

panels.description: Sum of voting_power across validators that are currently online and synced (catching_up=false). A reporting sentinel with a fresh sample is required.
panels.title: Active voting power

panels.description: floor(total × 2/3) + 1 — the tendermint BFT quorum threshold (tm2/pkg/bft/types/vote_set.go:272).
panels.title: Required for consensus

panels.description: Positive = how much voting power can go offline before the chain stalls. Zero = one more drop breaks consensus. Negative = chain is stalled.
panels.title: Margin (active − required)

panels.description: Per-validator voting-power status. Colour reflects losing-this-validator impact: green = chain keeps consensus without it; orange = active validator whose loss drops us below quorum (SPOF); red = validator is in the set but offline.

properties.value: active_vp − own_power − required. ≥ 0: losing this validator keeps quorum (safe). < 0: SPOF. For an offline validator it tells you what the margin would be if they came back.

targets.expr: (max by (address) (sentinel_rpc_validator_set_power) * 0) + on(address) group_left() (last_over_time(sentinel_validator_online[30s]) or on(address) (max by (address) (sentinel_rpc_validator_set_power) * 0))

panels.title: Per-validator voting power
